title: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit focuses on creators and content-driven businesses, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and small local businesses. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: Visual automations, landing pages, commerce, sequences
comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, social media ads, list building

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller wanting to start for free and scale affordably
You need visual automation workflows and landing page builders integrated into your email platform
You want to sell digital or physical products directly through your email marketing tool
choose_b_if: You run a nonprofit or local business needing event management and SMS capabilities
You want built-in social media ad creation and management alongside email
You prefer a single platform with less focus on creator-specific features

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit is better for creators and content-driven businesses with its visual automations and landing pages, while Constant Contact excels for nonprofits and local businesses needing event management and SMS. ConvertKit has higher user ratings overall (4.4 vs 4.0 on G2).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Constant Contact?
faq.a: ConvertKit is cheaper for small operations with its free tier supporting up to 1,000 subscribers at no cost. However, Constant Contact starts at $12/month with no free tier, making it more affordable per feature once you need paid plans. Your choice depends on your subscriber count and budget.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports, so you can migrate your subscribers. ConvertKit has documented import processes and customer support to help with transitions. You'll want to map your tags and segments carefully during the migration to maintain proper segmentation.
